page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARM64 architecture, it features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs using the Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 30.32 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.47 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25 percent. The Geekbench single-core score of 1897.0 and text processing score of 2335.0 also demonstrate strong performance. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall within the average range, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 10093.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 13646.63. However, the instance shows weaker performance in cryptographic operations, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 1510.85, and poor prompt processing speeds for medium-sized 7B LLM models.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps, supporting network-intensive workloads. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer a balanced compute-to-memory ratio for workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. This profile is highly suitable for compute-bound tasks, single-threaded data compression, text processing, static web serving, and lightweight containerized applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, particularly in single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench text processing, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. Cryptographic performance is a notable bottleneck, as indicated by a lower PassMark encryption score. This server is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads, single-threaded data processing, static web serving, and lightweight containerized applications that benefit from dedicated ARM64 cores without requiring local SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 16 GB of RAM, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and high-speed networking for efficient single-threaded and text processing workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 at 2.8 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Strong single-core performance in text processing and data compression
EBS-only storage configuration with no local SSDs or GPU accelerators
Suitable for compute-bound tasks, static web serving, and containerized apps
